text: Polymarket has Jesus returning this year at higher odds than $MSTR beating earnings  🤣

SpaceXAI announced a deal giving Anthropic access to Colossus 1 which is its AI supercomputer with 220,000+ $NVDA GPUs.

Anthropic will use the added capacity to improve Claude Pro and Max while also flagging interest in future orbital AI compute.

$NVDA is up over 5% today as the market finally rewards Jensen’s AI infrastructure dealmaking.

These deals are basically a roadmap for where shortages are getting worse and which bottlenecks matter most:

• Compute Capacity: $CRWV, $NBIS, OpenAI, Anthropic
• Optical Interconnect: $COHR, $LITE , $GLW

My full $AMD earnings takeaway can be found here:

piped.video/N2kcG3WbbUQ?si=f3QY…

$AMD is up over 15% today because this was the quarter where the market started reframing AMD from “the cheaper $NVDA alternative” into a true beneficiary of the next AI infrastructure phase where CPUs & GPUs both matter.

AMD has real shot at becoming a $1T company this year.
Berkshire Hathaway’s $BRK.B ~$397B cash pile is now larger than $PLTR entire ~$345B market cap  🤯

$TSLA & SpaceX are reportedly proposing a $55B investment (potentially rising to $119B) to build Elon Musk’s “Terafab” semiconductor facility. 

The facility would aim to produce 2nm chips for Musk’s robotics, space & AI projects with full-scale capacity supporting up to 1 terawatt of compute per year.

OpenAI partnered with $NVDA, $AMD, $AVGO, $INTC and $MSFT to develop MRC which a new protocol for large AI training clusters.

The goal is to improve GPU networking performance and resilience as AI infrastructure scales.

$OSS up over 40% after crushing Q1 earnings

• Revenue $8.1M vs Est. $6.9M (+54% YoY growth) 
• EPS $0.01 vs Est. ($0.06)
• Gross Margin 52% vs Est. 41%

FY26 Guidance
• Revenue 23% vs. Est. 21%

OSS is becoming a pure-play ruggedized edge compute company tied to defense, autonomy & mission-critical AI which is why it sits in my “Physical AI” basket.

A $10K investment in $AMD when Lisa Su became CEO would be worth ~$1.3M today.

That is what happens when you have a leader who actually executes.

$AAPL is spending more than 10% of revenue on R&D for the first time in at least 30 years.

Apple remains one of the few companies positioned to capture consumer AI deployment through device-level integration and unmatched distribution.

$MSFT is reportedly considering delaying or abandoning its 2030 goal to match 100% of hourly electricity use with renewables as AI data center demand surges.

The review comes as rising AI power needs make natural gas and more flexible energy sourcing harder for Microsoft to avoid.

$NVDA received warrants to buy up to 15M $GLW shares at $180 as part of its long-term partnership with Corning.

Corning also issued Nvidia a pre-funded warrant for up to 3M additional shares tied to the deal. 

Nvidia +3% and Corning +15% on the news.

In today's Early Look: "Crazy #Quad2?"

How many people in our profession are going to say "this market's crazy" today?

Lots. It usually means they're missing the moves (or they don't realize they're the ones being crazy). 

One of the craziest assumptions you can have about markets is that they always make fundamental sense.

They don't. They make fractal sense. Those are very different things.

“Chaos theory explains why markets are unpredictable in detail yet disciplined in behavior.”
-Richard Brennan @RichB118

📺 DON’T OVERTHINK STRONG MARKETS + MY POWER PLAYS IN MEGA CAP TECH

The market $SPY $SPX is in a powerful uptrend, confirmed after reclaiming key moving averages and forming an inverted head & shoulders pattern.

Since that technical shift, momentum has stayed consistently strong, with price holding above the 8-day the entire move — a classic sign of a healthy trend.

This is not a market to fight — it’s a market to participate in.

*

Leadership has rotated back into mega-cap tech (Mag 7), which is a major tailwind for the broader market.

As a result, we had several Power Plays in mega-cap tech in the past several weeks.

Check out my Power Plays product –  t3live.com/power

1. $GOOGL
Google has been the best performer in my portfolio recently. 

Bullish signal triggered after reclaiming a head & shoulders structure. It had a strong move through the gap pivot (~$311) and earnings.

Up even more today, so I'm trimming into strength. It is a textbook execution: identify structure → enter → hold → scale out.

2. $AMZN
Amazon has not been as smooth as Google, but it still broke new highs. It delivered solid gains. 

Another example of buying vs. gap levels and letting trend work.

3. $AAPL 
We entered Apple (as a “Power Play”) during a tight consolidation. The strategy was to buy breakout + options and add on dips.

Post-earnings, it had a strong move higher. Some say there is a bearish double-top, but I disagree. I target ~$295+ in the coming weeks.

I am actively trading around the position and even planning to rotate capital (e.g., trimming Google and adding to Apple).

*

During this period, many traders underperformed or even blew up by trying to be “too smart”, over-hedged, or fighting the trend.

Meanwhile, simpler trend-following approaches have worked extremely well.

Follow the trend (don’t fight it), don’t cut winners too early, and stay active.

*

So, this is a textbook bullish environment driven by strong technical structure, mega-cap leadership, and persistent momentum.

Do not fight the market that is working!

*
